Step 11

Screenshot-based design-system review. This stage reviews the final design system section by section against the source screenshot and records score, weakest areas, and actionable learnings.

Sections
  • Design-system review prompt
  • Design-system review token usage
  • Design-system review token breakdown
  • Design-system review summary
  • Design-system review markdown
  • Design-system review JSON
Design-system review promptruns/v177/design-system-review-prompt.md
You are one review agent in a parallel design-system audit.

You will receive:
- One original source screenshot
- One focused section from the final design-system markdown
- Optional YAML front matter from the same design-system markdown as shared token context

Your job is to score how well this focused design-system section emulates the screenshot's reusable visual system. Evaluate the markdown section itself, not a generated website and not exact page reconstruction.

## Scoring

Use a strict 0-10 score:
- 10: the section captures the screenshot's reusable patterns with precise, implementation-useful rules
- 7-9: strong fit with small omissions or mild overgeneralization
- 4-6: partially useful, but important screenshot patterns are missing, vague, or unsupported
- 1-3: mostly generic, misleading, or weakly grounded in the screenshot
- 0: absent or unrelated

## Rules

- Focus only on the named design-system section. Use the YAML context only when it helps interpret tokens referenced by that section.
- Compare against the screenshot directly.
- Do not reward vague design prose unless it would help a generator reproduce the screenshot's visual system.
- Penalize unsupported exact values, section-specific role names, palette-specific general rules, or rules that would fail on a different site.
- Reward generic reusable roles and mechanics that faithfully encode the screenshot, such as surface relationships, hierarchy, nesting, contrast, component sizing behavior, typography rhythm, and layout grammar.
- Penalize violations of the Typography Normalization Contract: body/paragraph tokens outside `14px-16px`, subhead/lead/intro/supporting-heading tokens above `1.5x` body, text-bearing controls or text links smaller/larger than canonical body size, or semantic `h1`/`h2`/`h3` tokens that do not share one font family and one font weight.
- Do not penalize separate contextual display/decorative emphasis tokens when they are used only as emphasis spans or special display roles and the semantic `h1`/`h2`/`h3` contract remains intact.
- Reward design systems that move exact per-section layout order and one-off positions out of reusable rules, while preserving repeated layout patterns and page grammar.
- Reward separate imagery creative directions for `icons`, `illustrations`, `interfaces`, and `photography`, especially when each category records density, simplicity/detail level, rendering medium, surface relationship, and overcomplexity limits.
- Penalize image/graphic guidance that blends all visuals into one generic style or would generate complex illustrations when the source uses simple ones.

## v172 Freshness Review Addendum

- Penalize any design system that exposes source-section IDs, source-section labels, `derived_from`, `evidenceSections`, or run-order fields in the final artifact.
- Penalize `patterns.sections`, `patterns.surface_runs`, `source_order`, `page_sequence`, or `section_sequence` in generation-facing artifacts. Ask for unordered reusable archetypes and adjacency principles instead.
- Reward systems that preserve visual grammar while making it impossible for site generation to infer the exact original section order from token names, pattern names, or provenance.
- Keep findings concrete and implementation-oriented.
- Return JSON only, with no markdown fences.

Use this exact shape:

{
  "summary": "short focused summary",
  "score": 0,
  "confidence": 0,
  "accurate_patterns": ["bullet", "bullet"],
  "missing_or_weak_patterns": ["bullet", "bullet"],
  "overfit_or_unsupported_rules": ["bullet", "bullet"],
  "actionable_learnings": ["bullet", "bullet"],
  "verdict": "short verdict"
}

Step 12

Generation input handed to all models. This is the final shared input that gets fanned out in parallel so Claude, Gemini, and GPT-5.5 all generate from the same source of truth.

Sections

  • Site generation input

Website promptruns/v177/website-gen-prompt.md
You are an expert frontend developer. You will be given a text-based design system extracted from a website screenshot. Your job is to generate a complete, single-file HTML website that feels native to this design system.

Requirements:
- Output a SINGLE complete HTML file with all CSS inlined in a <style> tag
- If the design system includes YAML front matter tokens, treat those tokens as the normative source of truth and treat the markdown body as rationale and usage guidance
- If the design system is a pure YAML document, treat `tokens`, `surfaces`, `typography`, `components`, `patterns`, and `rules` as the normative implementation contract
- Use the design system's structural system, repeated layout grammar, surface families, typography system, shape/depth system, component rules, and four-category imagery direction
- If the design system or grounding includes a "Source Font Implementation" section or source `@font-face` CSS, include that CSS in the generated page, but apply typography by the design-system role tokens first. Do not let one broad source font stack override role-specific font families, heading consistency rules, or text-size normalization in the design system.
- When source font files are provided, do not substitute Google Fonts or a generic font family for the primary typography
- Preserve the Typography Normalization Contract from the design system in generated CSS: body/paragraph text stays `14px-16px`; subhead, lead, intro, and supporting-heading text stays at or below `1.5x` body; text-bearing buttons, nav links, footer links, tabs, and inline links use the canonical body size; semantic `h1`, `h2`, and `h3` share one font family and one font weight. Decorative/display emphasis spans may use contextual display/decorative tokens without replacing the semantic heading contract.
- Preserve grounded typography casing with CSS. If a typography token, component recipe, or grounding rule says a role is uppercase/all-caps, implement `text-transform: uppercase` on the corresponding class or element role; do not rely on hand-written placeholder copy being typed in uppercase.
- If a token or component recipe includes `textTransform`, `caseBehavior`, or an explicit casing rule, translate that into CSS for headings, card titles, labels, buttons, metadata, or links as appropriate.
- Treat the design system as a reusable system, not as a screenshot reconstruction
- Create a realistic, professional-looking landing page with placeholder content that matches the described system
- The page should have a navigation bar, a footer, and enough content sections to reflect the described page rhythm and module density
- Prefer roughly 6-9 sections when the design system describes a long or highly sectional page
- Each section should feel like it belongs to the same system while still being a fresh composition
- Do not copy the source screenshot's exact section list, exact section order, or exact component positions. Use only recurring layout tendencies and surface grammar from the design system unless an explicit separate `layouts.yaml` artifact is intentionally supplied for source reconstruction.
- Vary section composition within the design system: reuse repeated layout patterns such as centered intro stacks, two-column splits, wide rails, card rails, inset panels, and selector rows, but do not reproduce the original page as a one-to-one sequence. Do not use exact source section order or one-off positions unless `layouts.yaml` is explicitly supplied for reconstruction.
- Preserve the dominant container behavior, spacing rhythm, alignment discipline, and layout tension from the design system
- Preserve recurring parent/child surface ownership, boundary behavior, and inset-panel patterns rather than collapsing everything into isolated generic sections
- Preserve grounded surface transition mechanics. If the design system describes a white/near-white-to-tint wash entering a closing area, start the gradient from the previous white/near-white canvas color before easing into the tint; do not start the gradient from an already-saturated tint.
- Use the component base recipes consistently
- When a component has contextual variants by surface family, only use the listed variants that fit the chosen surface
- Do not invent a stronger or brighter secondary button style than the primary on the same surface
- Only use secondary, ghost, tertiary, or text-button variants when they are explicitly defined in the design system
- If the design system says the button hierarchy is `single-primary-button pattern`, do not invent a filled secondary button; use either one primary CTA plus a text link, or the explicitly defined low-emphasis secondary treatment
- If the design system marks a secondary button as `derived`, keep it lower emphasis than the primary using ghost, low-opacity fill, subtle tonal fill, or dim border logic from the same surface family
- Preserve component sizing behavior. Foreground images, image placeholders, logos, icons, buttons, CTA pills, eyebrows, badges, tags, chips, text links, and compact metadata labels should size intrinsically or to an explicit bounded media frame; they should never become full-width just because their parent container is full-width.
- Implement content-hugging controls with CSS that defeats parent stretch, especially inside column flex stacks where `align-items: stretch` is the default. Use `display: inline-flex`, `width: max-content` or `fit-content`, `max-width: 100%`, `white-space: nowrap`, `flex: 0 0 auto`, plus `align-self` and `justify-self` that match the intended alignment. Do not rely on `inline-flex` and `width:auto` alone, because those can still stretch inside flex-column parents.
- If an older design system says `width:auto` in a `cssSizingHint` for a content-hugging button, eyebrow, badge, chip, pill, tag, CTA, or metadata label, treat that as incomplete legacy shorthand. Implement the control with `width: max-content` or `width: fit-content` plus explicit non-stretch alignment instead.
- Treat `width: 100%` on foreground images, logos, eyebrows, badges, tags, chips, text links, compact labels, or ordinary buttons as a mistake unless the element is filling a deliberately sized media frame that is itself the bounded object. Size the frame deliberately, then let the image fill that frame; do not let the image or compact text element span the entire card, column, or section by default.
- For content stacks that contain compact controls, either set the parent stack alignment to the intended non-stretch alignment (for example `align-items: flex-start` or `align-items: center`) or set explicit `align-self` on each compact control.
- Do not put one global `align-self:flex-start` on all compact labels, badges, chips, or pills. A compact control inside a centered intro/CTA stack must center with that stack; a compact control inside a left-aligned stack may align start. Alignment is contextual even when the intrinsic sizing recipe is shared.
- Do not use `display: block`, `width: 100%`, `justify-self: stretch`, parent `align-items: stretch`, or implicit flex-column stretch on pill-like buttons, eyebrows, badges, tags, chips, or compact metadata labels unless full-width sizing is explicitly grounded. If mobile wrapping is needed, wrap the text or the control group, not the individual pill width.
- Preserve compact icon-only/circular action recipes when the design system defines them. If a card, tile, carousel, nav, or footer pattern calls for a circle with an arrow/icon inside, render the circle control itself with fixed square dimensions rather than replacing it with a loose arrow glyph or text-only link.
- Preserve recurring content stacks such as eyebrow + heading + paragraph + CTA when the design system says they are common
- Keep recurring card families distinct; do not mix many unrelated card background colors unless the design system explicitly supports that
- Treat rare or one-off motifs as optional accents, not mandatory patterns
- Do NOT invent extra component families, extra accent colors, or extra layout tricks that are not supported by the design system
- Make it responsive using CSS Grid/Flexbox
- Use CSS custom properties to reflect the surface tokens and component role tokens described in the design system
- Do not use JavaScript frameworks for app structure. JavaScript is allowed only for GSAP motion and small plain-JS glue code. Do not use shader, WebGL, Three.js, procedural canvas, or particle systems for bespoke generated imagery.
- The page should look polished and complete, not like a style guide
- Simple single-color UI/supporting icons must use Phosphor-style inline SVGs, not generated images. Use icons equivalent to the Phosphor icon set (https://phosphoricons.com/) and inject the SVG markup directly into the HTML; do not load an external icon font, sprite, script, or remote SVG. Apply `fill="currentColor"` to SVG fills and `stroke="currentColor"` to strokes when strokes are present so each icon inherits the surrounding text/icon color. Keep `width`, `height`, `viewBox`, stroke/fill weight, corner language, and optical density consistent with `imagery.icons`.
- Do not create `<img data-stt-asset-brief>` placeholders for simple one-color icons such as arrows, menu/search/cart/user glyphs, checkmarks, plus/minus, social glyphs, or small support pictograms. Render them as inline Phosphor/currentColor SVGs inside the appropriate button, link, badge, or icon container.
- Only multi-color pictorial icons that visibly require multiple colors, gradients, dimensional treatment, or non-library artwork may become generated image placeholders, and their `data-stt-asset-brief` must include `multi-color-icon`.
- Do not generate image assets for logos, wordmarks, payment marks, customer marks, or brand emblems. Render logo slots as live text when appropriate, or as a simple rectangle/block using the body/text color for that surface with the observed approximate footprint. Do not add `data-stt-asset-brief` to logo placeholders.
- Treat `imagery.icons`, `imagery.illustrations`, `imagery.interfaces`, and `imagery.photography` as the source of truth for visual style, density, simplicity, and rendering complexity. The subject matter for each generated asset must come from the local component/section slot, nearby content, and `data-stt-asset-brief`, not from the original screenshot subject.
- Do not overcomplicate generated assets. If `imagery.illustrations` says sparse/simple/low-detail, placeholders and downstream briefs must ask for sparse/simple/low-detail illustrations even when the slot subject is different. Apply the same density/simplicity discipline to interface mockups and photography.
- For larger graphics, illustrations, decorative visuals, portraits, collages, or photo-like panels, do NOT use stock-photo URLs or final baked imagery; instead output blank image placeholders whose aspect ratio matches the intended layout/context
- When the design system or grounding marks a graphic/image as `background-blended`, seamless, softly masked, or part of a section background, implement it as a section background image placeholder/layer rather than a foreground media block. Preserve any reserved visual zone by giving the background layer enough clear space and positioning foreground text away from detailed subject matter when the source does that.
- When the design system or grounding marks a visual as concrete-edged, clipped, framed, foreground-media, foreground-graphic, or embedded-showcase, implement it as a foreground `<img>`, SVG placeholder, card/media frame, or embedded visual as appropriate, with visible bounds matching the described edge behavior.
- For every generated visual placeholder except simple icons and logos, use an <img> element with a blank data URI or empty inline SVG placeholder source, size it correctly in the layout, keep an accessible plain-language `alt`, and include a `data-stt-asset-brief` attribute that describes the local subject matter plus one of these categories: `photography`, `illustration`, `interface`, or `multi-color-icon`.
- The `data-stt-asset-brief` should be concise but specific enough for downstream image generation: local subject/content, category, mood, framing, material/texture, color treatment, density/simplicity level, edge behavior, and design-system style. Do not copy source-site subject matter when fresh placeholder content calls for a different subject.
- Every `photography` asset brief must say that the photo content fills the entire generated image edge-to-edge. Explicitly prohibit an inner card, inset poster, padded thumbnail, matte, border, white margin, drop-shadow frame, or extra background plate inside the generated image. If the design system calls for a rounded media frame, border, card, or clipping mask, implement that frame in CSS around the `<img>`; do not ask the image model to paint the frame into the photo.
- Prefer `<img data-stt-asset-brief="...">` placeholders for foreground and concrete-edged generated visuals so downstream `gpt-image-2` asset generation can fill them. Use CSS `background-image: url(...)` or an absolutely positioned background-layer placeholder for visuals that the design system identifies as blended section backgrounds, atmospheric plates, textures, or seamless illustrations.
- IMPORTANT: Do NOT use viewport units (100vh, 100svh, min-height: 100vh) on sections or containers. Sections should size naturally based on their content. Only the hero section may optionally use a max-height but never min-height: 100vh.
- Keep the CSS concise — avoid overly verbose or redundant styles

Output ONLY the HTML code, no explanations or markdown fences.

## Site Generation Layout Freshness

- Do not copy the source screenshot's exact section list, exact section order, or exact component positions.
- Use broad layout tendencies and surface grammar from the design system, but make a fresh page composition unless a separate `layouts.yaml` artifact is explicitly provided and requested for source reconstruction.
- Use only repeated layout patterns from `patterns.layout`; treat exact per-section layout data as intentionally externalized to `layouts.yaml`.
- Keep grounded shared parent surface behavior from turning into new full-width section resets; neutral, tinted, inverse, or contrasting fills should remain child/inset foreground modules only when that is what the design system says.
- Center compact eyebrows/pills inside centered intro or CTA stacks; do not apply a global `align-self:flex-start` to every compact label.
- Use defined circular icon-action variants as actual circles with centered arrows/icons, not loose arrow glyphs.
- For those circular or square icon-action variants, put the Phosphor/currentColor inline SVG inside the fixed-size control; do not turn the icon into an image placeholder.
- Start white-to-tint transition gradients from the prior white/near-white canvas color before easing into the tint.
- Do not use shader, WebGL, Three.js, procedural canvas, or particle systems for bespoke generated imagery. Use explicit `data-stt-asset-brief` placeholders so downstream `gpt-image-2` asset generation can fill visual wells.
- In each `data-stt-asset-brief`, carry over the relevant imagery category's style, density, simplicity, and edge/framing direction from the design system while letting subject matter follow the new component slot.
- For `photography` briefs, restate edge-to-edge image content and keep page-level frames in CSS only.

## v172 Fresh Composition Hard Gate

- Treat the design system as an unordered pattern library. Do not use the order of surfaces, tokens, components, or patterns in the artifact as page order.
- Internally choose a fresh section plan before writing HTML. Navigation and footer may remain conventional bookends; the body must not be a source-page reconstruction with renamed content.
- Do not render every observed source archetype. Select a smaller coherent subset of reusable patterns and vary their order, density, and local composition.
- Do not place repeated rails, inset trays, proof/stat rows, inverse media bands, editorial media mosaics, review rails, utility strips, and service bars in the same relative source order just because all are available as patterns.
- If the design system names a one-off or contextual page moment, use it only when it naturally serves the fresh page being created; never treat it as a required section.
- Output only the final HTML. Do not include a visible explanation of this planning.
Site style sync promptruns/v177/site-style-sync-prompt.md
You are correcting a generated single-file HTML document so its explicit visual values match the source-of-truth CSS styles and the provided design-system markdown.

Rules:
- Preserve the existing HTML structure, content, and layout as closely as possible.
- Focus on correcting explicit CSS values only: colors, gradients, font-family, font-size, font-weight, line-height, and letter-spacing.
- Use the source CSS style report as authoritative for exact explicit values.
- If the design-system markdown includes an explicit exact value and that value is supported by the source CSS style report, prefer it.
- Preserve the design-system Typography Normalization Contract when correcting typography: source CSS font values are evidence, not an override, for body/paragraph `14px-16px`, subhead/lead/intro/supporting-heading max `1.5x` body, text-link/control body-size matching, and semantic `h1`/`h2`/`h3` shared font family and weight.
- Do not invent new hex/rgb/rgba/hsl colors, gradients, or font-family stacks.
- Do not introduce approximate alpha variants, blended shades, or softened contrast values that are not supported by the source CSS style report.
- If an explicit visual value is unsupported, replace it with the closest supported explicit value from the design-system markdown or source CSS style report.
- Preserve existing component width behavior while correcting explicit visual values. Do not introduce `display: block`, `width: 100%`, or stretch alignment on content-hugging buttons, eyebrows, badges, tags, chips, or compact metadata labels.
- Do not rewrite layout, markup, or copy unless that is required to keep the HTML valid.
- Return ONLY the corrected full HTML document.
Site generation inputruns/v177/2025-12-12_88730-roma-hotel-and-restaurant-framer-website-template/single/site-generation-input.md
# Site Generation Input

## Source Design System

schema_version: design_system_yaml.v1
type: design_system
metadata:
  name: warm_editorial_linework_system
  description: "A warm paper-toned editorial system with deep green ink, high-contrast serif display type, architectural hairlines, compact inverse controls, and hard-edged photography."
  confidence: high

tokens:
  color:
    surface:
      primary:
        value: "rgb(249,247,239)"
        role: warm paper canvas
        confidence: high
      inverse:
        value: "rgb(38,78,51)"
        role: dark green control or ink-filled surface
        confidence: high
      transparent:
        value: "rgba(255,255,255,0)"
        role: same-surface child fill
        confidence: high
      externalOverlay:
        value: "rgb(255,255,255)"
        role: non-site fixed overlay only
        confidence: low
    text:
      primary:
        value: "rgb(38,78,51)"
        role: deep green ink on warm canvas
        confidence: high
      onInverse:
        value: "rgb(249,247,239)"
        role: warm ivory text and icons on dark green controls
        confidence: high
      externalOverlay:
        value: "rgb(34,34,34)"
        role: non-site overlay text only
        confidence: low
    border:
      muted:
        value: "rgb(38,78,51)"
        role: softened green architectural hairlines on same-fill panels
        confidence: medium
      strong:
        value: "rgb(38,78,51)"
        role: deep green outline for compact circular controls or badges
        confidence: high
      onInverse:
        value: "rgb(249,247,239)"
        role: ivory segmentation lines on dark controls
        confidence: high
      none:
        value: "transparent"
        role: no visible border
        confidence: high
    accent:
      primary:
        value: "rgb(38,78,51)"
        role: primary action fill and graphic ink
        confidence: high
      onPrimary:
        value: "rgb(249,247,239)"
        role: action text and icon on primary fill
        confidence: high
    graphic:
      photographicWarm:
        value: "warm amber, cream, ochre, olive, deep green-black, brown shadow"
        role: photography palette direction, not CSS token
        confidence: high
      photographicAccent:
        value: "occasional teal, red, lime, and gold pixels"
        role: local photo accent pixels, not UI color
        confidence: medium
      brandInk:
        value: "rgb(38,78,51)"
        role: single-color marks and wordmark placeholders
        confidence: high
  typography:
    body:
      value: "{typography.body}"
    control:
      value: "{typography.control}"
    caption:
      value: "{typography.caption}"
    heading:
      value: "{typography.h2}"
    display:
      value: "{typography.display_contextual}"
  spacing:
    0: "0"
    1: "4px"
    2: "8px"
    3: "12px"
    4: "16px"
    5: "20px"
    6: "24px"
    8: "32px"
    10: "40px"
    12: "48px"
    16: "64px"
    20: "80px"
    24: "96px"
    roles:
      edgeInsetSmall: "16px-24px"
      wideRail: "clamp(24px, 6vw, 88px)"
      sectionAir: "clamp(72px, 12vw, 160px)"
      panelPadding: "clamp(20px, 4vw, 48px)"
      cellPadding: "clamp(18px, 3vw, 36px)"
      compactControlPadding: "8px 12px"
      iconButton: "32px"
      captionGap: "16px-19px"
      mediaGap: "18px-24px"
      looseCollageGap: "clamp(48px, 10vw, 160px)"
  radius:
    none:
      value: "0"
      confidence: high
    controlSmall:
      value: "2px-3px"
      confidence: medium
    circle:
      value: "999px"
      confidence: high
    externalOverlay:
      value: "7px"
      confidence: low
  shadow:
    none:
      value: "none"
      confidence: high
    externalOverlay:
      value: "0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.14)"
      confidence: low
  divider:
    hairlineMuted:
      width: "1px"
      style: "solid"
      color: "{tokens.color.border.muted}"
      confidence: high
    hairlineStrong:
      width: "1px"
      style: "solid"
      color: "{tokens.color.border.strong}"
      confidence: high
    hairlineOnInverse:
      width: "1px"
      style: "solid"
      color: "{tokens.color.border.onInverse}"
      confidence: high
    hardMediaEdge:
      width: "0"
      style: "hard boundary made by adjacent raster image edge"
      confidence: high
  motion:
    durationFast: "120ms"
    durationBase: "180ms"
    durationSlow: "320ms"
    easing: "cubic-bezier(0.22, 1, 0.36, 1)"
    reducedMotion: "disable non-essential transform motion"

surfaces:
  warm_canvas:
    value: "rgb(249,247,239)"
    role: page_canvas
    text:
      default: "{tokens.color.text.primary}"
      muted: "{tokens.color.text.primary}"
      accent: "{tokens.color.text.primary}"
    border: "none"
    shadow: "{tokens.shadow.none}"
    gradient: "none"
    usage:
      - "Primary full-width warm paper surface for brand/editorial bookends and same-surface modules."
      - "May carry a very subtle low-contrast paper grain; do not convert this into a strong texture."
    confidence: high
  inverse_control_surface:
    value: "rgb(38,78,51)"
    role: control
    text:
      default: "{tokens.color.text.onInverse}"
      muted: "{tokens.color.text.onInverse}"
      accent: "{tokens.color.text.onInverse}"
    border: "{tokens.divider.hairlineOnInverse}"
    shadow: "{tokens.shadow.none}"
    gradient: "none"
    usage:
      - "Compact filled controls and segmented control clusters on warm canvas or same-fill panels."
    confidence: high
  same_fill_panel:
    value: "rgb(249,247,239)"
    role: inset_panel
    text:
      default: "{tokens.color.text.primary}"
      muted: "{tokens.color.text.primary}"
      accent: "{tokens.color.text.primary}"
    border: "{tokens.divider.hairlineMuted}"
    shadow: "{tokens.shadow.none}"
    gradient: "none"
    usage:
      - "Inset modules separated from the canvas by 1px green linework, not by elevation."
    confidence: high
  transparent_on_warm:
    value: "rgba(255,255,255,0)"
    role: other
    text:
      default: "{tokens.color.text.primary}"
      muted: "{tokens.color.text.primary}"
      accent: "{tokens.color.text.primary}"
    border: "none unless inherited from parent linework"
    shadow: "{tokens.shadow.none}"
    gradient: "none"
    usage:
      - "Text, graphics, cells, and link cards that sit directly on warm canvas or same-fill panels."
    confidence: high
  photo_owned_surface:
    value: "raster photo pixels"
    role: media_host
    text:
      default: "none"
      muted: "none"
      accent: "none"
    border: "none"
    shadow: "photographic shadows only, not CSS elevation"
    gradient: "natural photo luminance falloff only"
    usage:
      - "Full-bleed media bands where the raster image owns the visible surface."
      - "Unframed square or portrait media where photo pixels reach the media bounds."
    confidence: high
  external_overlay_surface:
    value: "rgb(255,255,255)"
    role: other
    text:
      default: "{tokens.color.text.externalOverlay}"
      muted: "{tokens.color.text.externalOverlay}"
      accent: "{tokens.color.text.externalOverlay}"
    border: "none"
    shadow: "{tokens.shadow.externalOverlay}"
    gradient: "none"
    usage:
      - "Visible capture/tooling overlay only; not part of generated page UI."
    confidence: low

typography:
  body:
    fontFamily: "\"Switzer\", \"Inter\", sans-serif"
    fontFamilyCategory: sans_serif
    visualCharacteristics:
      - compact
      - clean
      - editorial utility
    fontSize: "14px"
    fontWeight: "400"
    lineHeight: "1.45"
    letterSpacing: "0"
    textTransform: none
    role: "canonical body, values, metadata values, and normal text-bearing UI fallback"
    confidence: high
  body_medium:
    fontFamily: "\"Switzer\", \"Inter\", sans-serif"
    fontFamilyCategory: sans_serif
    visualCharacteristics:
      - compact
      - slightly firm
    fontSize: "14px"
    fontWeight: "500"
    lineHeight: "1.25"
    letterSpacing: "0"
    textTransform: none
    role: "compact card/body copy while respecting normalized body sizing"
    confidence: medium
  control:
    fontFamily: "\"Switzer\", \"Inter\", sans-serif"
    fontFamilyCategory: sans_serif
    visualCharacteristics:
      - compact
      - centered
      - utilitarian
    fontSize: "14px"
    fontWeight: "500"
    lineHeight: "1"
    letterSpacing: "0"
    textTransform: none
    role: "buttons, compact action labels, nav labels, and text-bearing controls"
    confidence: high
  label_upper:
    fontFamily: "\"Switzer\", \"Inter\", sans-serif"
    fontFamilyCategory: sans_serif
    visualCharacteristics:
      - narrow
      - compact
      - editorial
    fontSize: "14px"
    fontWeight: "400"
    lineHeight: "1.15"
    letterSpacing: "0.02em"
    textTransform: uppercase
    role: "eyebrows, metadata labels, and uppercase micro links"
    confidence: high
  caption:
    fontFamily: "\"Switzer\", \"Inter\", sans-serif"
    fontFamilyCategory: sans_serif
    visualCharacteristics:
      - narrow
      - terse
      - caption-like
    fontSize: "14px"
    fontWeight: "400"
    lineHeight: "1.15"
    letterSpacing: "0.02em"
    textTransform: uppercase
    role: "media captions and handle-like captions"
    confidence: high
  h1:
    fontFamily: "\"Bonny\", \"Instrument Serif\", serif"
    fontFamilyCategory: serif
    visualCharacteristics:
      - high_contrast
      - condensed
      - editorial
    fontSize: "56px"
    fontWeight: "400"
    lineHeight: "0.95"
    letterSpacing: "-0.02em"
    textTransform: uppercase
    role: "semantic h1"
    confidence: medium
  h2:
    fontFamily: "\"Bonny\", \"Instrument Serif\", serif"
    fontFamilyCategory: serif
    visualCharacteristics:
      - high_contrast
      - condensed
      - editorial
    fontSize: "44px"
    fontWeight: "400"
    lineHeight: "1"
    letterSpacing: "-0.015em"
    textTransform: uppercase
    role: "semantic h2 and common large section heading"
    confidence: high
  h3:
    fontFamily: "\"Bonny\", \"Instrument Serif\", serif"
    fontFamilyCategory: serif
    visualCharacteristics:
      - high_contrast
      - condensed
      - editorial
    fontSize: "31px"
    fontWeight: "400"
    lineHeight: "1.05"
    letterSpacing: "-0.01em"
    textTransform: uppercase
    role: "semantic h3 and compact section/panel heading"
    confidence: high
  display_contextual:
    fontFamily: "\"Bonny\", \"Instrument Serif\", serif"
    fontFamilyCategory: serif
    visualCharacteristics:
      - extreme_scale
      - high_contrast
      - wordmark_like
      - sparse
    fontSize: "clamp(96px, 18vw, 280px)"
    fontWeight: "400"
    lineHeight: "0.78"
    letterSpacing: "-0.04em"
    textTransform: uppercase
    role: "contextual oversized identity or bookend display; not a semantic heading default"
    confidence: high

components:
  segmented_control_panel:
    kind: panel
    actualPageUI: true
    confidence: high
    anatomy:
      - "single dark rectangular cluster"
      - "optional blank upper zone"
      - "bottom row of compact labels"
      - "icon-only segment separated by 1px divider"
      - "internal ivory hairline grid"
    base:
      typography: "{typography.control}"
      padding: "0"
      radius: "{tokens.radius.none}"
      display: grid
      widthBehavior: content_hugging
      cssSizingHint: "display:inline-grid; width:max-content; max-width:100%; white-space:nowrap; flex:0 0 auto;"
      textTransform: none
    variants:
      inverse_on_warm:
        surface: inverse_control_surface
        backgroundColor: "{tokens.color.surface.inverse}"
        textColor: "{tokens.color.text.onInverse}"
        borderColor: "{tokens.color.border.onInverse}"
        dividerColor: "{tokens.color.border.onInverse}"
        iconColor: "{tokens.color.text.onInverse}"
        shadow: "{tokens.shadow.none}"
        contrastSource: fill_contrast
    doNotUseFor:
      - "full-width navigation bars"
      - "large menu drawers"
      - "floating dark overlays"
  compact_button:
    kind: button
    actualPageUI: true
    confidence: high
    anatomy:
      - "short text label"
      - "optional adjacent icon segment managed by parent compound control"
    base:
      typography: "{typography.control}"
      padding: "{tokens.spacing.roles.compactControlPadding}"
      radius: "{tokens.radius.controlSmall}"
      display: inline-flex
      widthBehavior: content_hugging
      cssSizingHint: "display:inline-flex; width:max-content; max-width:100%; white-space:nowrap; flex:0 0 auto; align-items:center; justify-content:center;"
      textTransform: none
    variants:
      filled_on_warm:
        surface: warm_canvas
        backgroundColor: "{tokens.color.accent.primary}"
        textColor: "{tokens.color.accent.onPrimary}"
        borderColor: "transparent"
        dividerColor: "transparent"
        iconColor: "{tokens.color.accent.onPrimary}"
        shadow: "{tokens.shadow.none}"
        contrastSource: fill_contrast
      filled_on_same_fill_panel:
        surface: same_fill_panel
        backgroundColor: "{tokens.color.accent.primary}"
        textColor: "{tokens.color.accent.onPrimary}"
        borderColor: "transparent"
        dividerColor: "transparent"
        iconColor: "{tokens.color.accent.onPrimary}"
        shadow: "{tokens.shadow.none}"
        contrastSource: fill_contrast
    doNotUseFor:
      - "large rounded CTA pills"
      - "full-width mobile buttons unless locally required"
      - "secondary outlined button systems without evidence"
  split_action_control:
    kind: other
    actualPageUI: true
    confidence: medium
    anatomy:
      - "content-hugging text button"
      - "adjacent square icon button"
      - "optional subtle internal divider"
    base:
      typography: "{typography.control}"
      padding: "0"
      radius: "{tokens.radius.controlSmall}"
      display: inline-flex
      widthBehavior: content_hugging
      cssSizingHint: "display:inline-flex; width:max-content; max-width:100%; white-space:nowrap; flex:0 0 auto; overflow:hidden;"
      textTransform: none
    variants:
      filled_compound_on_warm_or_panel:
        surface: warm_canvas
        backgroundColor: "{tokens.color.accent.primary}"
        textColor: "{tokens.color.accent.onPrimary}"
        borderColor: "transparent"
        dividerColor: "#F9F7EFBF"
        iconColor: "{tokens.color.accent.onPrimary}"
        shadow: "{tokens.shadow.none}"
        contrastSource: fill_contrast
    doNotUseFor:
      - "large promotional buttons"
      - "multi-icon toolbars"
  icon_button:
    kind: icon_button
    actualPageUI: true
    confidence: high
    anatomy:
      - "single currentColor SVG icon"
      - "fixed square or circular hit area"
      - "no text label rendered visually"
    base:
      typography: "{typography.control}"
      padding: "0"
      radius: "{tokens.radius.none}"
      display: inline-flex
      widthBehavior: icon_only
      cssSizingHint: "display:inline-flex; width:32px; height:32px; min-width:32px; max-width:32px; flex:0 0 auto; align-items:center; justify-content:center;"
      textTransform: none
    variants:
      inverse_segment:
        surface: inverse_control_surface
        backgroundColor: "{tokens.color.surface.inverse}"
        textColor: "{tokens.color.text.onInverse}"
        borderColor: "{tokens.color.border.onInverse}"
        dividerColor: "{tokens.color.border.onInverse}"
        iconColor: "{tokens.color.text.onInverse}"
        shadow: "{tokens.shadow.none}"
        contrastSource: same_surface_border
      filled_compact:
        surface: warm_canvas
        backgroundColor: "{tokens.color.accent.primary}"
        textColor: "{tokens.color.accent.onPrimary}"
        borderColor: "transparent"
        dividerColor: "transparent"
        iconColor: "{tokens.color.accent.onPrimary}"
        shadow: "{tokens.shadow.none}"
        contrastSource: fill_contrast
      outline_circle_on_warm:
        surface: warm_canvas
        backgroundColor: "transparent"
        textColor: "{tokens.color.text.primary}"
        borderColor: "{tokens.color.border.strong}"
        dividerColor: "transparent"
        iconColor: "{tokens.color.text.primary}"
        shadow: "{tokens.shadow.none}"
        contrastSource: same_surface_border
      bare_inline_on_warm:
        surface: transparent_on_warm
        backgroundColor: "transparent"
        textColor: "{tokens.color.text.primary}"
        borderColor: "transparent"
        dividerColor: "transparent"
        iconColor: "{tokens.color.text.primary}"
        shadow: "{tokens.shadow.none}"
        contrastSource: whitespace
    doNotUseFor:
      - "photo overlays"
      - "social logo generation"
      - "multi-color pictorial icons"
  ordinal_badge:
    kind: compact_label
    actualPageUI: true
    confidence: high
    anatomy:
      - "centered short numeral or marker"
      - "transparent fill"
      - "1px circular outline"
    base:
      typography: "{typography.control}"
      padding: "0"
      radius: "{tokens.radius.circle}"
      display: inline-flex
      widthBehavior: fixed_size
      cssSizingHint: "display:inline-flex; width:24px; height:24px; min-width:24px; flex:0 0 auto; align-items:center; justify-content:center; max-width:100%; white-space:nowrap;"
      textTransform: none
    variants:
      outline_on_warm_cell:
        surface: transparent_on_warm
        backgroundColor: "transparent"
        textColor: "{tokens.color.text.primary}"
        borderColor: "{tokens.color.border.strong}"
        dividerColor: "transparent"
        iconColor: "{tokens.color.text.primary}"
        shadow: "{tokens.shadow.none}"
        contrastSource: same_surface_border
    doNotUseFor:
      - "filled notification badges"
      - "brand emblems"
  same_surface_panel:
    kind: panel
    actualPageUI: true
    confidence: high
    anatomy:
      - "same-fill rectangular panel"
      - "1px muted green outline"
      - "optional header divider"
      - "optional internal grid dividers"
    base:
      typography: "{typography.body}"
      padding: "0"
      radius: "{tokens.radius.none}"
      display: block
      widthBehavior: parent_stretched
      cssSizingHint: "display:block; width:100%; max-width:100%;"
      textTransform: none
    variants:
      hairline_on_warm:
        surface: same_fill_panel
        backgroundColor: "{tokens.color.surface.primary}"
        textColor: "{tokens.color.text.primary}"
        borderColor: "{tokens.color.border.muted}"
        dividerColor: "{tokens.color.border.muted}"
        iconColor: "{tokens.color.text.primary}"
        shadow: "{tokens.shadow.none}"
        contrastSource: same_surface_border
    doNotUseFor:
      - "elevated cards"
      - "alternate-tint section backgrounds"
      - "rounded containers"
  same_surface_link_cell:
    kind: card
    actualPageUI: true
    confidence: high
    anatomy:
      - "transparent cell surface"
      - "small title or body text"
      - "optional bare currentColor arrow"
      - "unframed media"
      - "separation supplied by parent dividers"
    base:
      typography: "{typography.body_medium}"
      padding: "{tokens.spacing.roles.cellPadding}"
      radius: "{tokens.radius.none}"
      display: flex
      widthBehavior: parent_stretched
      cssSizingHint: "display:flex; width:100%; max-width:100%; flex-direction:column;"
      textTransform: none
    variants:
      divided_on_same_fill_panel:
        surface: same_fill_panel
        backgroundColor: "transparent"
        textColor: "{tokens.color.text.primary}"
        borderColor: "inherited parent dividers"
        dividerColor: "{tokens.color.border.muted}"
        iconColor: "{tokens.color.text.primary}"
        shadow: "{tokens.shadow.none}"
        contrastSource: divider
      divided_on_warm_grid:
        surface: transparent_on_warm
        backgroundColor: "transparent"
        textColor: "{tokens.color.text.primary}"
        borderColor: "inherited parent dividers"
        dividerColor: "{tokens.color.border.muted}"
        iconColor: "{tokens.color.text.primary}"
        shadow: "{tokens.shadow.none}"
        contrastSource: divider
    doNotUseFor:
      - "filled cards"
      - "shadowed cards"
      - "rounded image cards"
  media_frame:
    kind: media_frame
    actualPageUI: true
    confidence: high
    anatomy:
      - "raster image"
      - "hard rectangular clipping"
      - "no internal padding"
      - "no overlay"
    base:
      typography: "{typography.body}"
      padding: "0"
      radius: "{tokens.radius.none}"
      display: block
      widthBehavior: intrinsic_media
      cssSizingHint: "display:block; overflow:hidden; line-height:0; flex:0 0 auto;"
      textTransform: none
    variants:
      square_unframed:
        surface: photo_owned_surface
        backgroundColor: "raster photo pixels"
        textColor: "none"
        borderColor: "transparent"
        dividerColor: "transparent"
        iconColor: "none"
        shadow: "{tokens.shadow.none}"
        contrastSource: whitespace
      portrait_unframed:
        surface: photo_owned_surface
        backgroundColor: "raster photo pixels"
        textColor: "none"
        borderColor: "transparent"
        dividerColor: "transparent"
        iconColor: "none"
        shadow: "{tokens.shadow.none}"
        contrastSource: whitespace
      full_bleed_band:
        surface: photo_owned_surface
        backgroundColor: "raster photo pixels"
        textColor: "none"
        borderColor: "transparent"
        dividerColor: "transparent"
        iconColor: "none"
        shadow: "{tokens.shadow.none}"
        contrastSource: none
    doNotUseFor:
      - "device mockups"
      - "photo cards with matte frames"
      - "rounded image masks unless newly evidenced"
  text_link:
    kind: link
    actualPageUI: true
    confidence: high
    anatomy:
      - "plain text"
      - "optional currentColor arrow nearby"
      - "no underline by default"
    base:
      typography: "{typography.control}"
      padding: "0"
      radius: "{tokens.radius.none}"
      display: inline-flex
      widthBehavior: content_hugging
      cssSizingHint: "display:inline-flex; width:max-content; max-width:100%; white-space:nowrap; flex:0 0 auto; align-items:center;"
      textTransform: preserve_authored_case
    variants:
      green_on_warm:
        surface: transparent_on_warm
        backgroundColor: "transparent"
        textColor: "{tokens.color.text.primary}"
        borderColor: "transparent"
        dividerColor: "transparent"
        iconColor: "{tokens.color.text.primary}"
        shadow: "{tokens.shadow.none}"
        contrastSource: whitespace
      ivory_on_inverse:
        surface: inverse_control_surface
        backgroundColor: "transparent"
        textColor: "{tokens.color.text.onInverse}"
        borderColor: "transparent"
        dividerColor: "{tokens.color.border.onInverse}"
        iconColor: "{tokens.color.text.onInverse}"
        shadow: "{tokens.shadow.none}"
        contrastSource: fill_contrast
    doNotUseFor:
      - "blue browser-default links"
      - "underlined editorial links unless required locally"

patterns:
  layout:
    - name: full_width_warm_canvas_with_inset_foreground
      confidence: high
      rule: "Use a viewport-wide warm canvas with foreground content aligned to broad rails or sparse edge insets; transparent children inherit the canvas rather than introducing new fills."
    - name: same_surface_hairline_module
      confidence: high
      rule: "Build grids and panels as same-fill rectangles with 1px green outer linework and internal dividers; structure comes from borders, not elevation or card backgrounds."
    - name: centered_intro_above_wide_field
      confidence: high
      rule: "A compact centered label or uppercase serif heading may sit above a wider grid, panel, or freeform media field with generous vertical breathing room."
    - name: compact_inverse_control_cluster
      confidence: high
      rule: "Small filled dark green controls sit as content-hugging objects on warm surfaces; labels and icon segments are divided by ivory hairlines."
    - name: unframed_media_pixels_to_edges
      confidence: high
      rule: "Media frames clip photos to square, portrait, or viewport-wide rectangles with zero radius, zero shadow, zero border, and no internal padding."
    - name: oversized_display_microcopy_contrast
      confidence: high
      rule: "Pair extremely large high-contrast serif identity/display regions with tiny utilitarian text groups or compact controls, separated by whitespace."
    - name: irregular_editorial_collage
      confidence: medium
      rule: "When using a collage, keep it sparse and manually staggered with large negative space, square hard-edged photos, and centered captions; do not regularize into a card grid."
    - name: full_bleed_photo_reset
      confidence: high
      rule: "A raster image may interrupt the warm canvas as a hard-edged viewport-width band with no text overlay, no scrim, no border, no radius, and no CSS shadow."
  page_moments:
    - name: opening_bookend_behavior
      confidence: high
      rule: "An opening brand moment may use the warm canvas continuously with small foreground marks or inverse controls before a hard reset into full-bleed media."
    - name: closing_bookend_behavior
      confidence: high
      rule: "A closing brand moment may return to the warm canvas with sparse microcopy, monochrome marks, and oversized contextual display type; avoid cards, forms, or dividers unless newly evidenced."
    - name: photo_interlude_behavior
      confidence: high
      rule: "Photo interludes are image-owned bands; adjacent warm-canvas slivers are boundary/neighbor surfaces, not a photo-section background token."
    - name: inset_child_panel_behavior
      confidence: high
      rule: "Inset panels remain child modules on the warm canvas; their same-fill background must not be promoted into a full-width section reset."
  content_composition:
    - name: divided_same_surface_cells
      confidence: high
      rule: "Use equal cells separated by inherited 1px hairlines; cells may contain compact labels, body copy, currentColor arrows, and unframed photos."
    - name: media_caption_unit
      confidence: high
      rule: "A media unit is a hard-edged image with a small uppercase or handle-like caption below; no card chrome surrounds the pair."
    - name: sparse_microcopy_grid
      confidence: high
      rule: "Microcopy groups distribute across columns using whitespace only; labels are uppercase, values preserve authored case where needed."
    - name: panel_header_action_row
      confidence: high
      rule: "Panel headers can pair a left-aligned uppercase serif heading with a content-hugging compact filled action pair."
  adjacency_principles:
    - name: parent_surface_ownership
      confidence: high
      rule: "Determine parent canvas from viewport-edge and gutter continuity; same-color child panels and cards do not create new parent surfaces."
    - name: hard_reset_vs_child_media
      confidence: high
      rule: "A full-bleed photo owns the surface only when it reaches the viewport edges; photos inside cells, cards, and collages remain child media."
    - name: linework_separation
      confidence: high
      rule: "Same-surface modules use borders/dividers for separation; do not add fill contrast, shadows, or rounded corners."
    - name: unordered_generation
      confidence: high
      rule: "These patterns are reusable mechanics and do not imply a required page sequence."
  background_systems:
    - name: warm_paper_canvas
      confidence: high
      rule: "Use a flat warm off-white canvas with deep green foreground; optional paper grain must be extremely subtle and low contrast."
    - name: absent_css_gradient_system
      confidence: high
      rule: "Do not create global CSS gradients from photographic light falloff; gradients observed in photos belong to raster imagery."
    - name: green_inverse_blocks
      confidence: high
      rule: "Dark green blocks are compact controls only unless locally grounded as a larger surface."
    - name: hard_photo_edges
      confidence: high
      rule: "Photo resets begin and end with hard horizontal edges, not divider lines or fade washes."
  image_graphics:
    - name: icon_asset_routing
      confidence: high
      rule: "Single-color UI icons are not image-generation candidates and should be inline currentColor SVGs; multi-color pictorial icons may be generated only if explicitly introduced; logos and wordmarks are not generated assets."
    - name: brand_mark_handling
      confidence: high
      rule: "Use fixed logo/wordmark slots or simple text/rectangle placeholders in current surface ink; do not infer reusable illustration style from marks."
    - name: photo_generation
      confidence: high
      rule: "Generated photos should fill their asset edge-to-edge; page components provide clipping, aspect ratio, and placement."
    - name: no_embedded_ui_promotion
      confidence: high
      rule: "Observed live navigation, buttons, panels, and cards are real components; no interface screenshot or device mockup style was observed."
  responsive:
    - name: rail_compression
      confidence: medium
      rule: "Wide rails compress from generous desktop insets to 20px-24px mobile gutters while preserving full-width warm surfaces."
    - name: divided_modules_on_small_screens
      confidence: medium
      rule: "Hairline grids may stack into one or two columns; preserve outer border and internal divider logic rather than converting to individual cards."
    - name: compact_controls
      confidence: high
      rule: "Compact controls remain content-hugging; do not stretch them to full width unless a local mobile layout explicitly requires it."
    - name: oversized_display_scaling
      confidence: high
      rule: "Contextual display type must scale with clamp() and may wrap/split, but should retain sparse, wordmark-like dominance."

imagery:
  icons:
    observed: true
    iconAssetClasses:
      - single-color-icon
      - logo/wordmark
    generationStrategy: mixed
    creativeDirection: "Minimal single-color currentColor SVG-style menu, arrow, and utility glyphs; logo and wordmark slots are monochrome fixed assets or live-type placeholders, not generated illustrations."
    density: sparse
    simplicity: simple
    rendering: line
    paletteRelationship: "Deep green on warm ivory, or warm ivory on dark green controls; no reusable multi-color icon palette observed."
    surfaceRelationship: "Icons sit directly on transparent warm surfaces, inside dark compact controls, inside circular outline controls, or as uncontained link arrows."
    edgeAndScale: "Small, flat, unshadowed; fixed icon buttons around 32px, circular outline controls around 32px, bare arrows sized to text context."
    implementationRule: "Use Phosphor-style inline SVG with stroke/fill driven by currentColor for single-color-icon; route logo/wordmark to no generated asset."
    subjectPolicy: "style from design system; subject from local component slot"
    avoid:
      - "multi-color pictograms"
      - "detailed illustrative icons"
      - "photo-derived icons"
      - "generated logo or wordmark art"
      - "external overlay glyphs as page UI"
  illustrations:
    observed: false
    creativeDirection: "No reusable illustration system is present; leave illustration slots empty unless a new local requirement provides direction."
    density: sparse
    simplicity: minimal
    rendering: none_observed
    paletteRelationship: "N/A; do not derive illustration colors from photography or logos."
    surfaceRelationship: "N/A."
    edgeAndScale: "N/A."
    subjectPolicy: "style from design system; subject from local component slot"
    avoid:
      - "character scenes"
      - "3D scenes"
      - "abstract vector decoration"
      - "turning brand marks into illustrations"
      - "decorative pictorial icons"
  interfaces:
    observed: false
    creativeDirection: "No product/interface imagery or device mockups observed; page UI remains live components."
    density: sparse
    simplicity: minimal
    rendering: none_observed
    paletteRelationship: "N/A."
    surfaceRelationship: "N/A."
    edgeAndScale: "N/A."
    subjectPolicy: "style from design system; subject from local component slot"
    avoid:
      - "dashboard screenshots"
      - "device frames"
      - "browser mockups"
      - "promoting live page controls into image assets"
  photography:
    observed: true
    creativeDirection: "Warm editorial/documentary photography with natural texture, directional light, cinematic shadow contrast, shallow depth or close crops, tactile surfaces, and organic imperfection."
    density: moderate
    simplicity: moderate
    rendering: editorial
    paletteRelationship: "Warm amber, cream, ochre, olive, deep green-black, deep brown shadow, and occasional teal/red/lime accents harmonize with the warm ivory and deep green UI; photo colors are not CSS tokens."
    surfaceRelationship: "Photos either own full-bleed viewport bands or sit as child media in square/portrait frames, collage units, or divided cells."
    edgeAndScale: "Photo pixels reach the asset edges; no internal matte, poster border, CSS shadow, or rounded image corners."
    assetEdgeBehavior: edge_to_edge_photo_content
    subjectPolicy: "style from design system; subject from local component slot"
    avoid:
      - "stock-like isolated cutouts"
      - "internal image padding"
      - "rounded photo corners"
      - "CSS photo shadows"
      - "overlay scrims on photo bands"
      - "treating photographic luminance falloff as a CSS gradient"

rules:
  color:
    - "Use rgb(249,247,239) as the primary warm canvas and rgb(38,78,51) as the primary ink/action color."
    - "Preserve host-surface pairings: deep green ink on warm canvas; warm ivory text/icons on deep green controls."
    - "Use muted green hairlines only for same-fill panels and divided modules; do not add default borders to high-contrast filled controls."
    - "Do not use source blue link defaults for generated page UI; they are not grounded in the visual system."
    - "Treat warm/dark photographic colors as imagery direction, not reusable UI colors."
  typography:
    - "Canonical body, compact body, captions, links, buttons, and text-bearing controls use 14px."
    - "Semantic h1, h2, and h3 share the same serif family and 400 weight; hierarchy changes by size, line-height, and placement."
    - "Oversized wordmark-like display type is contextual and must not replace semantic heading tokens."
    - "Uppercase casing is grounded for serif headings, display lockups, metadata labels, captions, and many micro links."
    - "Preserve authored case for values, card titles, and compact action labels unless the local text role is explicitly uppercase."
  spacing:
    - "Use generous vertical air around editorial modules and large whitespace around contextual display type."
    - "Use wide rails for inset panels/grids; full-bleed photo bands ignore rails."
    - "Keep compact controls content-hugging with tight padding and fixed/icon-only sizing where applicable."
    - "Place caption gaps around 16px-19px when media captions sit below hard-edged photos."
  containers:
    - "Section shells, rows, grids, rails, collage fields, and divider scaffolds are patterns, not standalone components."
    - "Same-fill panels should remain square, flat, and line-defined."
    - "A child panel or media well must not be promoted to a parent background unless it visibly reaches the viewport edges."
  components:
    - "Content-hugging controls must use inline-flex, width:max-content or fit-content, max-width:100%, white-space:nowrap, and flex:0 0 auto."
    - "Do not bake global align-self behavior into compact labels, buttons, chips, or icon buttons; center them in centered stacks and start-align them in left-aligned stacks via parent layout."
    - "Icon-only controls require fixed dimensions and explicit icon color; do not collapse them into text arrows."
    - "Segmented inverse panels use dark fill plus ivory divider linework; do not turn them into a full-width dark header."
  cards:
    - "Transparent same-surface cells are not elevated cards; separation comes from parent dividers."
    - "Do not add card fills, shadows, or radius to divided grid/link cells."
    - "Unframed media inside cells remains a media child with pixels to edges."
  links_actions:
    - "Text links are plain currentColor text with no default underline; optional arrows are single-color currentColor SVGs."
    - "Filled compact actions use deep green fill, warm ivory label/icon, tiny radius, and no shadow."
    - "Circular carousel-style icon buttons use transparent fill, 1px deep green outline, and deep green currentColor arrows."
  imagery_graphics:
    - "Single-color icons are inline currentColor SVGs and are not image-generation candidates."
    - "Multi-color icons are not part of the observed system; only generate them if a new local slot explicitly requires pictorial multi-color treatment."
    - "Logos, wordmarks, customer marks, payment marks, and brand emblems are non-generated assets; use simple placeholders in current ink when exact art is unavailable."
    - "Illustrations are not observed; do not introduce character, 3D, or abstract vector scenes."
    - "Photography should be warm, tactile, cinematic, and edge-to-edge within the generated asset."
  motion_animation:
    - "Use restrained system-native hover/focus transitions: color, opacity, and very small translate changes under 180ms."
    - "Avoid parallax, shader/canvas effects, elaborate scroll animations, or motion that changes hard photo edges and surface ownership."
    - "Icon buttons may animate icon opacity or a 1px-2px translate on hover, but must preserve fixed dimensions."
    - "Respect reduced-motion by disabling non-essential transforms."
  accessibility:
    - "Maintain sufficient contrast between deep green text and warm ivory canvas, and between ivory text and deep green controls."
    - "Icon-only controls need accessible labels even when no text is visible."
    - "Hairline-only structure should not be the only cue for interactive state."
    - "Do not reduce text-bearing controls below the canonical 14px size."

do_not_generalize:
  - "Do not treat the external white/black fixed overlay badges as page UI."
  - "Do not infer a full-width dark navigation bar from the compact inverse control cluster."
  - "Do not convert same-surface divided cells into filled, rounded, or shadowed cards."
  - "Do not add overlays, borders, padding, radius, or shadows to full-bleed photo bands."
  - "Do not tokenize photographic shadows, bokeh, fabric texture, or luminance falloff as CSS effects."
  - "Do not require the optional irregular collage mechanic on every page."
  - "Do not reconstruct a source page sequence from these reusable patterns."
  - "Do not introduce illustration or interface mockup systems without new evidence."
  - "Do not use external blue link colors from browser/source defaults."

open_questions:
  - "Exact production font mapping for all small sans roles is partly ambiguous; Switzer/Inter is the safest normalized utility family."
  - "Subtle paper grain may be a real background asset or screenshot/compression noise; keep it optional and extremely low contrast."
  - "Some compact filled action controls may have a subtle internal icon divider; confidence is medium outside clearly segmented controls."
  - "Navigation cluster corner notches or tiny corner details are low-confidence and should not be required."
  - "Boundary slivers near photo resets may belong to adjacent warm canvases rather than the photo-owned surface."
source_font_implementation:
  guidance: "Use the downloaded source font files for generated HTML rather than substituting a guessed web font."
  primaryStack: "sans-serif"
  decorativeItalicStack: "\"Inter\""
  css: |

      font-family: "Geist";

    :root { --stt-source-font-family: sans-serif; --stt-source-decorative-italic-font-family: "Inter"; }

    html, body, button, input, textarea, select { font-family: var(--stt-source-font-family) !important; }

    h1, h2, h3, h4, h5, h6, .display, .heading, .heading-lg, .heading-md, .heading-sm, [class*="display"], [class*="heading"] { font-family: var(--stt-source-font-family) !important; }

    h1 em, h2 em, h3 em, h4 em, h5 em, h6 em, .display em, .heading em, [class*="display"] em, [class*="heading"] em, .emphasis, .italic, [class*="emphasis"], [class*="italic"] { font-family: var(--stt-source-decorative-italic-font-family) !important; font-style: italic; }

## Fresh Composition Contract

- Treat the design system above as an unordered library of reusable surfaces, components, imagery styles, and layout grammar.
- Do not infer page order from the order of tokens, surfaces, components, patterns, or evidence in this artifact.
- Build a fresh landing-page composition: navigation and footer may remain conventional bookends, but the body sections must be selected and ordered as a new composition.
- Preserve reusable surface ownership, component recipes, typography, imagery style, and spacing rhythm without reconstructing the source page's section sequence.
- If an artifact still contains a trace hint, treat it only as non-rendered metadata and do not use it for class names, section names, or page order.

## Active Site Generation Skills

Use these skills as additional implementation guidance. The source design-system tokens and grounded visual rules remain authoritative when a skill and the source artifact conflict.

### motion-design-gsap

---
name: motion-design-gsap
description: Adds website motion design guidance for generated single-file HTML sites. Use when a generated page should include tasteful animation, scroll choreography, entrance motion, or micro-interactions using GSAP as the only animation framework. Not for CSS-only static pages or non-website animation tasks.
---

# Motion Design With GSAP

## Goal

Create motion that expresses the provided design system's energy without changing its layout, hierarchy, or component recipes. Motion should make the generated site feel finished, not like an animation demo.

## Implementation Rules

- Use GSAP as the only animation framework. Small plain JavaScript for selectors, guards, and event wiring is allowed.
- Include GSAP from a CDN in the single HTML file when animation is implemented.
- Use GSAP plugins only when they are part of the public GSAP distribution, such as ScrollTrigger. Do not rely on paid Club GSAP plugins.
- Respect `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`: disable timeline motion, scroll-triggered movement, and looping ambient effects while preserving readable final states.
- Do not animate layout-critical properties that cause reflow. Prefer `opacity`, `transform`, CSS variables, and shader/canvas uniforms.
- Keep motion subtle enough that the page remains inspectable in a pipeline iframe screenshot.

## Motion Selection

1. Read the design system's stated rhythm, density, visual tension, and surface behavior.
2. Choose one motion attitude:
   - Calm systems: short fades, low-distance y movement, slow ambient drift.
   - Editorial or premium systems: staggered reveals, restrained parallax, text/image timing offsets.
   - Technical or futuristic systems: measured scan, orbit, grid, or data-flow motifs.
   - Playful systems: snappier easing, small overshoot, responsive hover/tap feedback.
3. Apply motion to recurring site structures, not every element.

## Required Patterns

- Initialize elements in final readable states for users without JavaScript.
- Add a motion-prep runtime hook in the document head before the first stylesheet can paint. Do not add the first `.js`/motion class from a footer script after the page has already rendered.
- For entrance reveals, prefer a safe three-step pattern: early head hook + CSS hidden prep state, `gsap.set()` to copy that start state inline once GSAP is available, then `gsap.to()` to animate to the final readable state. This avoids visible-then-hidden-then-visible flashes.
- Use `autoAlpha` or paired `opacity` and `visibility` for prepared hidden states so hidden interactive elements are not focusable/clickable before reveal. Always remove the prep hook and restore final states if GSAP is unavailable or reduced motion is requested.
- Use timeline labels or small named setup functions when more than one sequence exists.
- Keep entrance animations short: usually 0.45s-0.9s.
- Stagger repeated cards, stats, logos, or feature rows by small intervals.
- For hover/tap micro-interactions, animate only the interactive target and its local accent.
- Give each element one entrance-animation owner. Do not target the same element with both a generic utility reveal and a group stagger, and do not run multiple tweens that write competing `opacity`, `visibility`, or `transform` start values to the same element.

## No-FOUC Entrance Template

Use this structure, adapted to the page's actual selectors and distances, when adding scroll or load reveals:

```html
<head>
  <script>
    document.documentElement.classList.add('motion-prep');
  </script>
  <style>
    html.motion-prep [data-reveal] {
      opacity: 0;
      visibility: hidden;
      transform: translateY(24px);
    }
    @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) {
      html.motion-prep [data-reveal] {
        opacity: 1;
        visibility: visible;
        transform: none;
      }
    }
  </style>
</head>
```

```js
(function () {
  var root = document.documentElement;
  var reduce = window.matchMedia('(prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)').matches;
  var revealEls = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll('[data-reveal]'));

  function showFinalState() {
    root.classList.remove('motion-prep');
    revealEls.forEach(function (el) {
      el.style.opacity = '';
      el.style.visibility = '';
      el.style.transform = '';
    });
  }

  if (reduce || !window.gsap || !revealEls.length) {
    showFinalState();
    return;
  }

  gsap.registerPlugin(ScrollTrigger);
  gsap.set(revealEls, { autoAlpha: 0, y: 24 });
  root.classList.remove('motion-prep');

  revealEls.forEach(function (el) {
    gsap.to(el, {
      autoAlpha: 1,
      y: 0,
      duration: 0.65,
      ease: 'power2.out',
      scrollTrigger: {
        trigger: el,
        start: 'top 88%',
        once: true
      }
    });
  });

  window.addEventListener('load', function () {
    ScrollTrigger.refresh();
  });
})();
```

For grouped staggers, put `data-reveal-group` on the group and animate its children from one owner only. Do not also put `data-reveal` on those same children:

```js
gsap.utils.toArray('[data-reveal-group]').forEach(function (group) {
  var children = Array.from(group.children);
  gsap.set(children, { autoAlpha: 0, y: 18 });
  gsap.to(children, {
    autoAlpha: 1,
    y: 0,
    duration: 0.55,
    stagger: 0.08,
    ease: 'power2.out',
    scrollTrigger: { trigger: group, start: 'top 86%', once: true }
  });
});
```

## Avoid

- Framer Motion, Anime.js, AOS, ScrollReveal, Lottie libraries, or any animation framework besides GSAP.
- Giant page-loader animations that delay content.
- `gsap.from()` or `gsap.fromTo()` for ordinary opacity/transform entrance reveals on elements that may have already painted. From-type tweens render their start values immediately by default and can create a flash when scripts initialize late or when multiple tweens compete.
- Adding `.js`/motion CSS hidden states from a script near the end of `<body>`, which can produce a visible final state before the hidden state applies.
- Applying an entrance utility class to items that are also animated by a parent/group stagger.
- SplitText, MorphSVG, DrawSVG, or other paid GSAP plugins.
- Infinite motion on large text blocks or primary reading surfaces.
- Scroll effects that pin most of the page or fight natural document flow.

## Self-Check

Before outputting HTML:

- The page still works if JavaScript fails.
- Reduced-motion users get stable content.
- GSAP is the only animation framework.
- Motion reinforces the design system instead of adding a new aesthetic.
- There is no first-paint blink: animated elements are not visible in their final state before their entrance begins.
- Every entrance target has exactly one tween or timeline that owns its initial `opacity`/`visibility`/`transform` values.

Step 14

Generated image asset pass. This stage scans each model's rendered HTML for meaningful image, SVG, and CSS-background targets, builds a placement-aware image prompt from the design system, generates PNG assets, and rewrites the HTML to use them.

Sections
  • Asset token summary
  • Asset token breakdown
  • Claude asset prompts and images
  • GPT-5.5 asset prompts and images

Match its graphic medium, surface relationship, edge behavior, color roles, density, depth/shadow level, and allowed accents. Use the category creative direction for style, density, and simplicity only; derive the subject matter from the placement context and requested visual brief. Do not introduce off-palette status colors, glossy/beveled UI, extra frames, stock illustration tropes, or a different rendering style unless the design system explicitly asks for them.\n\nHard constraints: no readable text, no UI chrome, no browser frames, no watermarks, no stock-photo collage look, and no logos unless the placement explicitly requests a brand mark. Hard constraints: no readable text, no UI chrome, no browser frames, no watermarks, no stock-photo collage look, and no logos unless the placement explicitly requests a brand mark. The result must feel like an integrated website asset, not a standalone poster.

Version changelogruns/v177/changes.md
# v177 Changes

- Created `runs/v177` from the v176 prompt bundle.
- Limited the run inputs to `2025-12-12_88730-roma-hotel-and-restaurant-framer-website-template` and `petspan`.
- Uses the updated runtime defaults: GPT-5.5/high for analysis and section detection, with resolved model settings persisted into the version folder at runtime.
- Keeps final site generation providers from v176: Claude Opus 4.6 and GPT-5.5.
- Started as a repair run for v176, whose section crops used the previous default Claude section detector and produced bad section bounds.
- Ran `./venv/bin/python run_pipeline.py --version v177 --screenshots-dir "runs/v177/input-screenshots"`.
- Completed both requested sites with `run_complete: completed`: Roma and Petspan.
- Verified resolved runtime files show `openai`, `gpt-5.5`, and `high` for both analysis and section detection.
- Generated both final site variants for each site: `site-claude.html` and `site-gpt55.html`.
- Generated custom site assets for both final providers: Roma Claude 10 / GPT-5.5 8, Petspan Claude 6 / GPT-5.5 5.
- Regenerated `viewer.html` and wrote `viewer-data/v177.js`.
- Removed the post-generation `inject_source_font_css_into_html()` path from the pipeline and deleted the unused injector helper.
- Removed the previously injected `data-stt-source-fonts` override block from Roma `site-claude.html` and `site-gpt55.html`, allowing their generated `--font-heading: "Bonny", "Instrument Serif", serif` stacks to apply directly.
- Removed the disabled Gemini output column from viewer rendering and regenerated `viewer.html`.
- Added viewer support for optional grounding-generated sidecar sites (`site-claude-grounding.html` and `site-gpt55-grounding.html`) so the output iframes switch to grounding-derived sites when the Grounding tab is selected.
- Generated grounding-based GPT-5.5 sidecar sites for both v177 inputs without deleting or overwriting the existing design-system-generated `site-claude.html` and `site-gpt55.html` outputs.
- Wrote grounding site-generation inputs beside the originals: `site-generation-input.grounding.md` for Roma and Petspan.
- Generated custom assets for the grounding GPT-5.5 sidecars and retried failed image slots until no `data-stt-asset-brief` placeholders remained: Roma 10 images under `generated-assets/site-gpt55-grounding/`, Petspan 14 images under `generated-assets/site-gpt55-grounding/`.
- Updated `runs/v177/manifest.json` with optional `site_generation_input_grounding` and `site_gpt55_grounding` paths.
- Claude grounding sidecar generation completed for Roma only; wrote `site-claude-grounding.html`, intermediate/postpass audit files, and 15 generated assets under `generated-assets/site-claude-grounding/`.
- Updated `runs/v177/manifest.json` with the optional Roma `site_claude_grounding` path so the viewer can switch the Claude/Opus iframe when the Grounding tab is selected.
- Regenerated `viewer.html` and `viewer-data/v177.js` after adding the sidecar outputs.
- Applied a strict source HTML/CSS style post-pass to both grounding-based GPT-5.5 sidecar sites using `source-colors.json`, `source-style-ledger.yaml`, and the saved site-style sync prompt; remaining approximate fallback colors were forced to source-backed palette values.
- Verified both grounding sidecar sites now have zero unsupported colors, zero unsupported fonts, complete HTML, and no unfilled generated-asset placeholders.
